title: urochloa occidentalis
intro: Urochloa occidentalis, also known as Pala Pala, is a type of grass native to tropical regions of Australia, New Guinea, and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Urochloa occidentalis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and treat various infections. Urochloa occidentalis can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, or by making a decoction with the roots and rhizomes to harness its medicinal properties.
